question: Should detached compiler Buffers extend m65d-save or use a second named transaction entry?

composition.contract: m65d-save accepts String or Buffer and owns the existing COW/media transaction

dedicated.contract: m65d-save remains String-only and a second named entry owns Buffer payloads

common_cut: retire the preallocated FASL slot discovery and sector writer
copy one detached Buffer to the established staging window with the existing DMA seam
publish through M65D COW with media binding, denylist, readback verification, BAM claim and directory commit-last
retain all existing String-payload behavior and failure cases

critical_capacity_deltas.runtime_overlay_bank: nonnegative-headroom-only; every alignment credit must be attributed

decision_rule: Prefer composition when both variants satisfy the same transaction and critical-capacity gates; a second public entry must buy a measured property rather than merely duplicate payload dispatch.
claim_limit: This comparison may recommend an implementation cut. It does not promote product bytes, authorize capacity deltas, prove compile-string end-to-end on hardware, or reuse Wave-1 hardware receipts.
